blockingIssues.description: `is_dir_in_use()` in `.factory/init.sh` uses `lsof +d "$canon_dir"`, which may miss open files in nested subpaths; this can still allow pruning active derived-data directories and does not fully resolve the active-directory safety blocker.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document active-directory safety caveat for derived-data cleanup: `lsof +d` is non-recursive and can miss nested open files; use recursive checks (`lsof +D` or equivalent strategy) when safety depends on nested build-tree activity.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Fix review `misc-fix-deriveddata-prune-active-safety` reproduced a nested-file case where `lsof +d <candidate>` returned no PID while `lsof +D <candidate>` detected active use.

rejectedObservations.observation: Procedure-compliance evidence remains inconsistent with workers marking followedProcedure=true despite incomplete startup-read evidence.
Workers may attempt startup reads from `/Users/dewclaw/.factory/library/user-testing.md` instead of the repo-scoped `.factory/library/user-testing.md` path.
